description:
This map displays various land cover types within a defined Natura 2000 (N2K) Area of Interest (AOI), overlaid on a false-colour infrared satellite background image. The blue boundary line delineates the N2K AOI. Inside this boundary, polygons of different colours represent distinct land cover features: red areas likely indicate urban fabric or artificial surfaces; purple areas likely represent industrial, commercial, or transport units; yellow and light yellow areas typically denote agricultural fields or pastures; light green and dark green areas likely represent forests or other natural vegetation; and brown areas may represent sparsely vegetated land or wetlands.

The background satellite imagery uses false colours: healthy vegetation appears in shades of red/pink, urban areas and bare soil appear in light blue/cyan, and a river is visible as a dark blue/black feature in the bottom right corner. The map includes a scale bar indicating "1:50.000" and lengths of "0", "1", and "2 km", along with a North arrow pointing upwards. No explicit legend for the coloured land cover polygons is present in the image.
